hypothesis: Under DAC-relevant conditions (298 K, 0.4 mbar CO2, ~30% RH), branched polyamine polymers containing 30–50% secondary amine units and 20–40% primary amine units will exhibit ≥25% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake (mmol g−1) than linear polyamines of equal total amine density composed solely of primary amines, due to water-mediated cooperative binding between secondary amines and CO2.
